subject: skl_cloud-solution-architect-src-https-github-com-micr-fdee9a93-skills-cloud-solution-architect-skill-md_fdee9a93
evidence: cloud-solution-architect analysis: 'The agent loads this skill and walks through the 7-step review workflow: it picks an architecture style based on domain complexity and team autonomy, selects Azure services using the technology choice decision framework, applies relevant design patterns… validates against all five WAF pillars, and produces an Architecture Decision Record.' azure-cloud-migrate analysis: 'moving on-premises into Azure via Microsoft-prescribed lifecycle: discovery/assessment with Azure Migrate, wave planning, execution (lift-and-shift, re-platform, refactor), landing zone config, cost estimation, post-migration validation.' → chains_with, weight 0.73. The architect designs the target Azure architecture (service selections, WAF-validated patterns, ADRs) that the migration skill needs as input for landing zone configuration and workload placement decisions. Requires inference that greenfield designs translate to migration target designs, but service selection, landing zone patterns, and WAF compliance directly inform migration execution steps.

subject: skl_mcp-builder-src-https-github-com-microsoft-skills-d6d41f0c-423e-github-skills-mcp-builder-skill-md_d6d41f0c
evidence: mcp-builder (Microsoft) analysis: 'four-phase workflow guide for building MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers. It covers planning, implementation in three languages (TypeScript, Python, C#), testing, and creating evaluation question sets… deeply ties into Microsoft's own MCP server ecosystem, spending considerable real estate on Azure MCP, Foundry MCP, and Fabric MCP before you've written a single line of code.' mcp-builder (Anthropic) analysis: 'structured, four-phase development guide for building MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers… What distinguishes this skill is Phase 1's design philosophy: the tradeoff between comprehensive API coverage and workflow tools, naming conventions for discoverability, context management… Phase 4 — the evaluation system — is the most distinctive piece. The skill requires you to create 10 complex, read-only, multi-step questions that test whether an LLM can actually accomplish real tasks with your server.' → alternatives, weight 0.85. Both skills solve the same core task — guiding developers through building MCP servers — with the same four-phase structure (plan, implement, test, evaluate). The Microsoft version adds deep Azure ecosystem binding (48-service Azure MCP catalog, C#/.NET patterns, Entra ID auth, Foundry/Fabric) while the Anthropic version adds tool design philosophy depth and a rigorous evaluation framework. Pack synthesis would choose one based on platform: Microsoft ecosystem → MS version; general/TypeScript → Anthropic version.

subject: skl_skill-creator-src-https-github-com-microsoft-skill-f5dab2cc-3e-github-skills-skill-creator-skill-md_f5dab2cc
evidence: skill-creator (Microsoft) analysis: 'meta-skill: its job is to teach an AI agent how to create other skills, specifically Azure SDK wrapper skills conforming to Microsoft's internal skill repository conventions… a deeply institutional document encoding Microsoft's Azure authentication policies, directory layouts, testing harness expectations, and documentation update procedures. If you are inside Microsoft's microsoft/skills repo adding an Azure SDK skill, this is your required playbook. If you are anywhere else, most of this document is irrelevant noise.' skill-creator (Anthropic) analysis: 'meta-skill: a full development lifecycle for skill authoring, covering everything from initial intent capture through parallel test-case evaluation, benchmark scoring, blind A/B comparison, and description optimization for trigger accuracy… The core innovation is the parallel evaluation system: for each test case, the skill instructs the agent to spawn two subagents simultaneously — one with the skill loaded, one without — then grade outputs against assertions, compute benchmark stats… This is the single most ambitious skill-authoring guide I've seen.' → alternatives, weight 0.88. Both skills solve the same core task — teaching agents how to create skills (meta-skill authoring). The Microsoft version is a compliance checklist narrowly targeting Azure SDK skills within Microsoft's specific repo infrastructure (symlinks, pnpm harness, docs-site extraction, verbatim auth callout blocks). The Anthropic version is a general-purpose skill engineering framework with automated evaluation loops, benchmark scoring, and description optimization. Same task identity, fundamentally different scope, methodology, and audience. Pack synthesis would choose one based on context: inside Microsoft's skills repo → MS version; anywhere else → Anthropic version.
